execution_summary: We generated a Python 3.11 Docker image and executed the predefined analysis that tests whether higher risk aversion (average safe choices in PV and RV) is associated with fewer decision errors (LV dominance violations) in Bruner (2017) lab data. Initial execution failed with a segmentation fault due to SciPy/statsmodels native binaries. We replaced those dependencies with pure NumPy/Pandas implementations for Kendall’s tau-b, permutation p-values, and bootstrap CIs, then re-ran successfully. The results show a negative and statistically significant association, aligning with the preregistered hypothesis.

code_executed.logs: Base image python:3.11-slim; installed numpy, pandas, scipy, statsmodels, matplotlib, seaborn; image built without errors.
code_executed.environment: Docker image study-jae5q (Python 3.11-slim with numpy, pandas, scipy, statsmodels, matplotlib, seaborn)

code_executed.logs: Rebuilt image with tag analysis-exec to satisfy orchestrator runtime expectation.
code_executed.environment: Docker image analysis-exec (Python 3.11-slim with numpy, pandas, scipy, statsmodels, matplotlib, seaborn)

code_executed.logs: Initial execution failed: qemu: uncaught target signal 11 (Segmentation fault) - core dumped.
code_executed.environment: Container analysis-exec; SciPy/Statsmodels present (native code likely caused segfault under emulation).

code_executed.command: python data/analysis_entrypoint.py (after rewriting to pure NumPy/Pandas)
code_executed.logs: Analysis complete. N= 106; Kendall tau-b: -0.3580; one-sided p= 0.00033; 95% bootstrap CI: [-0.463, -0.229]. Pearson r: -0.4583; one-sided p= 0.00020; 95% bootstrap CI: [-0.600, -0.292]. Artifacts saved: results.json, table_correlations.csv, figure_scatter_fit.png.
code_executed.environment: Python 3.11 with numpy, pandas, matplotlib, seaborn (no SciPy/Statsmodels used in final run).

results.hypothesis_tested: Participants with higher risk aversion—operationalized as a greater average number of safe choices across PV and RV—will exhibit fewer decision errors—operationalized as a lower count of LV stochastic dominance violations.

findings_summary.outcome_name: Kendall’s tau-b between risk_avg_safe and decision_errors_lv
Kendall’s tau-b (PV-only) between risk1_sum and decision_errors_lv
Kendall’s tau-b (RV-only) between risk2_sum and decision_errors_lv
Pearson correlation between risk_avg_safe and decision_errors_lv

tables.table_description: Kendall’s tau-b (overall, PV-only, RV-only) and Pearson r with CIs and one-sided p-values.

figures.figure_description: Counts of duplicate observations overlaid with a linear fit of decision errors on average safe choices.
